free_text: Proton Strength Functions from (p,n) Cross Sections
Efficiency of BF3 neutron counter-system calibrated
by comparing experimental cross sections Of the
51V(p,n)51Cr reaction to values obtained with a
Mn bath.
Approved by A. Galonsky, 8 January 2001.
Data errors not given in McGowen compilation
but shown in the figure were added by the Karlsruhe
compiler.
Converted from data set B0046; updated at
NNDC. Originally compiled by KaChaPaG and updated by
CaJaD.
BP: Added doi, updated subentries 2-7,9,10
and 13 per WP2023-21.
OS. Corrections in sub.13
Natural NaCl target of 0.193 mg/cm**2, corresponding
to about 20 keV energy loss, evaporated onto 0.01
inch Pt backing.
Statistical uncertainty. Where no errors are
given, the errors are less than the point diameter in
the figures. Average standard error of about 10% from
all other sources, e.g., target thickness and
nonuniformity, beam measurement, detector efficiency,
and neglection of excited state neutrons.
Remeasurement of this reaction with thicker target
and with energies up to about 6 MeV.
BP: Updated REL-REF.
On. REL-REF -> STATUS

Natural Co target of 0.261 mg/cm**2, corresponding to
about 20 keV energy loss, evaporated onto 0.01 inch
Pt backing.
Data above 2.246 MeV are corrected in the publication
by 15% for the contribution of lower-energy neutrons
leading to excited states. This correction,
erroneously not applied in CPX, was performed by the
KACHAPAG compiler.
Statistical uncertainty. Where no errors are
given, the errors are less than the point diameter in
the figures. Average standard error of 10% from all
other sources, e.g., target thickness and
nonuniformity, beam measurement, detector efficiency,
and neglection of excited state neutrons.

and with energies up to about 6 MeV.
BP: Updated REL-REF.
On. REL-REF -> STATUS
Natural Cu target of 0.243 mg/cm**2, corresponding to
about 20 keV energy loss, evaporated onto 0.01 inch
Pt backing.
Data are obtained by assuming that all neutrons go to
the ground state. It is estimated by the authors that
the points at the upper energy limit should be
lowered by 5+-3% because of lower energy neutron
groups.
Statistical uncertainty. Where no errors are
given, the errors are less than the point diameter
in the figures.
Average standard error 10% from all other
sources, e.g., target thickness and nonuniformity,
beam measurement, detector efficiency, and neglection
of excited state neutrons.
Recent compilation by Colle et al.: present data are
compared to later measurements.
Remeasurement of this reaction with enriched and
thicker target and with energies up to about 6 MeV.

BP: Updated REL-REF (Entry B068 was
converted into T0126) per  WP2023-21.
On. REL-REF -> STATUS
Targets enriched to 49.4% 77Se evaporated onto 0.01
inch Pt backing, target thickness 0.240 and 0.218
mg/cm**2, respectively, corresponding to about 20 keV
energy loss.
Data are obtained by assuming that all neutrons go to
the ground state. It is estimated by the authors
that the points at the upper energy limit should be
lowered by 5+-3% because of lower energy neutron
groups.
Correction for:
. contribution of 3% 82Se in target (about 7%),
. target evaporation below 2450 keV.
Statistical uncertainty. Where no errors are
given, the errors are less than the point diameter
in the figures.
Average uncertainty in target thickness - 7%;
. due to 80Se (19.6%) and 82Se (3%) impurities,
. evaporation correction (5%).
Remeasurement of this reaction with energies up to
about 6 MeV.
Se-82-Targets Enriched to 75.74% Evaporated
Onto 0.01 Inch Pt-Backing, Target Thickness 0.175 and
0.185 Mg/Cm**2, Respectively, Corresponding to About
20 KeV Energy Loss.
Data below 1100 keV are corrected by 20% for target
evaporation losses.
Statistical uncertainty. Where no errors are
given, the errors are less than the point diameter
in the figures.
Average uncertainty in target thickness - 7%;
. due to target impurities,
. evaporation correction (5%).
Authors state that due to the large spin change (0+
to 6-), the transition to the ground state
contributes only very little to the measured cross
section. They assume the population of low-lying,
low-spin states in 82Br.
- BY KACHAPAG COMPILER - It is uncertain, if the
measured values are valid for the formation of 82(m)Br
(46 keV,2-) or if other low-lying states are also
populated (e.g., 78 keV, 1 or 2+).
Thick natural Nb target without backing
Derived from four thick target yields.
Cross sections given in the data section were
obtained by differentiating a thick-target excitation
function. Each data point results from 4 thick-target
yields.
Statistical uncertainty. Where no errors are
given, the errors are less than the point diameter in
the figures. Target error is the uncertainty in the
atomic stopping power for Nb of +-5%, found by
interpolation between Cu and Ag.
Remeasurement of this reaction with thicker target
and with energies up to about 6 MeV.
